behavior_summary: When the entity is inspected/looked at, the U8P_HAY::look process emits a single bark with the text "hay" and then the process suspends.
There is no branching, menuing, flag manipulation, or external calls; the routine is a minimal, self-contained U8P_HAY::look/inspect response.
The behavior likely runs in response to the engine's U8P_HAY::look/inspect event (processType 0x0208) and may also act as a fast-area/triggered U8P_HAY::look handler.
notable_control_flow: Linear sequence: bark (emit text) -> suspend/terminate the U8P_HAY::look process.
No conditional branches, loops, or calls to helper or external routines are present.
Function is implemented as a process (processType 0x0208) and ends immediately after producing the bark.
open_questions: Is this handler ever invoked as a fast-area 'enterFastArea' trigger (proximity) in addition to an explicit player 'U8P_HAY::look' action?
The raw disassembly included low-confidence 'Unknown' nodes (e.g., tokens like '{' and 'suspend'); are there any subtle side-effects encoded in those remnants or are they purely decompiler artifacts?
The stored text appears as "hay " (with trailing space) in samples — is the trailing space intentional for concatenation/formatting elsewhere?

main_function.name: U8P_HAY::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short/inspect response for this entity by emitting a single audible/text bark and then suspending the U8P_HAY::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine's U8P_HAY::look/inspect event (processType 0x0208).
May also be triggered when the player enters a relevant fast-area associated with this entity (inferred).
